title: Chapter 1 Page 5
text:
I watch as Gale pulls out his knife and slices the bread. He could be my brother. Straight black hair, olive skin, we even have the same gray eyes. But we’re not related, at least not closely. Most of the families who work the mines resemble one another this way. That’s why my mother and Prim, with their light hair and blue eyes, always look out of place. They are. My mother’s parents were part of the small merchant class that caters to officials, Peacekeepers, and the occasional Seam customer. They ran an apothecary shop in the nicer part of District 12. Since almost no one can afford doctors, apothecaries are our healers. My father got to know my mother because on his hunts he would sometimes collect medicinal herbs and sell them to her shop to be brewed into remedies. She must have really loved him to leave her home for the Seam. I try to remember that when all I can see is the woman who sat by, blank and unreachable, while her children turned to skin and bones. I try to forgive her for my father’s sake. But to be honest, I’m not the forgiving type.

Gale spreads the bread slices with the soft goat cheese, carefully placing a basil leaf on each while I strip the bushes of their berries. We settle back in a nook in the rocks. From this place, we are invisible but have a clear view of the valley, which is teeming with summer life, greens to gather, roots to dig, fish iridescent in the sunlight. The day is glorious, with a blue sky and soft breeze. The food’s wonderful, with the cheese seeping into the warm bread and the berries bursting in our mouths. Everything would be perfect if this really was a holiday, if all the day off meant was roaming the mountains with Gale, hunting for tonight’s supper. But instead we have to be standing in the square at two o’clock waiting for the names to be called out.

“We could do it, you know,” Gale says quietly.

“What?” I ask.

“Leave the district. Run off. Live in the woods. You and I, we could make it,” says Gale.

I don’t know how to respond. The idea is so preposterous.

“If we didn’t have so many kids,” he adds quickly.

They’re not our kids, of course. But they might as well be. Gale’s two little brothers and a sister. Prim. And you may as well throw in our mothers, too, because how would they live without us? Who would fill those mouths that are always asking for more? With both of us hunting daily, there are still nights when game has to be swapped for lard or shoelaces or wool, still nights when we go to bed with our stomachs growling.

quizlet: не из тех, кто легко прощает
он мог бы быть моим братом
all I can see is the woman who sat by
всё, что я вижу — женщину, которая бездействовала
all I can see is the woman who sat by
the cheese seeping into the warm bread
cheese seeping into the warm bread
mouths that are always asking for more
mouths that are always asking for more

questions.question: Why do Katniss’s mother and Prim look different from most Seam families?
options: they belong to the merchant class
they recently arrived from the Capitol
questions.explanation: Katniss explains that her mother came from the merchant class rather than the Seam.

questions.question: What does “turned to skin and bones” suggest?
questions.explanation: The phrase describes extreme hunger and physical deterioration.

questions.question: What does Katniss struggle to forgive her mother for?
options: emotionally shutting down after her father died
questions.explanation: Katniss remembers her mother becoming emotionally absent after her father's death.

questions.question: What does “teeming with summer life” emphasize?
options: the woods are full of life and food
questions.explanation: The phrase highlights abundance, beauty, and survival resources.

questions.question: Why does Gale suggest running away?
options: he believes they could survive in the woods
he wants to join another district
he wants to escape the reaping immediately
questions.explanation: Gale believes he and Katniss are skilled enough to survive outside District 12.

questions.question: Why can’t Katniss seriously consider leaving?
options: she is afraid of the woods
she feels responsible for her family
questions.explanation: Katniss feels deeply responsible for Prim, her mother, and their survival.
